---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

.size50[
Working on Paper 1

- Writing Workshop
]

<br>

.center[.size35[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2024)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. ...



---

class: middle, slidegreen

.size40[.content-box-green[**Paper 1: Introducing the Problem**]]

.size40[
Your report .textblue[**must**] include:

1. A description of the .textblue[**scientific basis**] for your problem,

2. An argument that this is a .textblue[**public problem**],

3. An argument that this requires a .textblue[**collective decision**], and

4. An analysis of .textblue[**at least two stakeholders**] with .textblue[**opposing**] viewpoints of the problem
]

???

This week we are working on your first paper

- This description is posted on Canvas with additional details

- Please see the "Paper Submission Requirements" in the syllabus

<br>

**SLIDE**: Before I let you loose on the writing workshop, let's talk grading.




---

class: middle, center, slidegreen

.size60[**To be convincing an argument should be .textred[logical], .textred[clear], .textred[credible] and .textred[critical].**]

???

This represents the heart of the rubric I use for grading written papers.

<br>

All of your written assignments in this class ask you to make arguments.

- Your goal for each is to write a convincing argument.

<br>

**SLIDE**: What does this mean?



---

class: middle, slidegreen

.size50[**Convincing arguments...**]

.size40[
+ .textred[**Logical**]: Do the premises support the conclusion?

+ .textred[**Clear**]: Is the paper easy to read and understand?

+ .textred[**Credible**]: Is there enough high quality evidence (cited using APA)?

+ .textred[**Critical**]: Does the paper address weaknesses in the argument (e.g. logic, evidence, consider counter-arguments)?
]

???

For grading, I basically read your paper and ask myself these four questions.

<br>

### Questions on the rubric or my expectations for your papers?




---

class: middle, slidegreen

.size40[.content-box-green[**Paper 1: Introducing the Problem**]]

.size40[
Your report .textblue[**must**] include:

1. A description of the .textblue[**scientific basis**] for your problem,

2. An argument that this is a .textblue[**public problem**],

3. An argument that this requires a .textblue[**collective decision**], and

4. An analysis of .textblue[**at least two stakeholders**] with .textblue[**opposing**] viewpoints of the problem
]

???

### With the rubric in mind, any questions on the first paper?

<br>

Today I want us to get and give feedback on a few of the projects in class.
- We'll reserve time at the end for writing/revision based on your work today

<br>

I want to emphasize that this is a SUPER valuable exercise
- Diving deeply into someone else's project will help you immeasurably with your own.

- See what works, what doesn't and help them find gaps in their arguments.

<br>

*Split Class into Pairs*

Ok, pairs, take 10 minutes to focus on ONE of the projects.
- Discuss where you are and then both of you spend the time focused on that project.

<br>

### Make sense?

- Go!

<br>

Ok, swap projects and now take 10 minutes on the other one!

<br>

*New pairs and repeat exercise!*
- 10 mins, person 1
- 10 mins, person 2

<br>

Let's now use the rest of our time to reflect on what you've done today and polish your own work!




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-forest_v3.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size70[.content-box-green[**For Next Class**]]

<br>

.center[.size55[
See the syllabus for readings on "command and control" policy designs
]]

???

Next week we shift into the next section of the class focused on policy design options

- e.g. what are some of the more popular or common approaches to solving environmental problems

<br>

We begin with "command and control" policies next week.
